description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from reddish-brown stone blocks, forming a single, prominent archway supported by two robust piers. The arch features a keystone at its apex and is flanked by decorative stonework, including protruding ledges and blocky elements at the base and midsection of each pier. Both sides of the arch are adorned with a series of upward-pointing, spear-like metal rods, adding a distinctive ornamental touch. The structure is solid and imposing, with a rough, weathered texture that suggests age and durability. The overall design is reminiscent of classical Roman triumphal arches, intended to serve as a monumental gateway or commemorative structure.
description: A reddish-brown stone triumphal arch with a single archway, blocky piers, and decorative spear-like metal rods on each side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway, keystone, and both sets of spear-like metal rods flanking the piers.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the arch and the protruding block elements on the piers.
description_view_2: Rear view of the arch, displaying the archway and the symmetrical arrangement of spear-like rods.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thickness of the structure and the side profile of the decorative elements.
